text: Our Chief Customer Officer and Interim Chief Network Officer @Matt Walsh took the stage at the Mid West Major Projects Conference yesterday to talk about Project Horizon and what metro-grade digital infrastructure means for the region. A few standouts from the session: ➡️Horizon isn't an isolated regional cable. It's a 2,000km extension of our national and international fibre network into the heart of the Mid West and Pilbara, deliberately routed inland to follow economic activity rather than the coastline. ➡️Hancock Iron Ore is already running around 50 autonomous haul trucks from a Perth control room over Horizon. The trucks are so precise they wear ruts into the road – the team had to introduce variability because the accuracy was too high. ➡️For decades, large parts of the Mid West and Pilbara have had a single long-haul fibre provider. Horizon changes that, introducing genuine choice. As Matt put it, the Mid West deserves infrastructure that matches the scale of what it contributes to this country. For the full picture on Project Horizon, watch Matt's on-demand webinar 'Inside Project Horizon' - https://lnkd.in/gVXxRZWA #ProjectHorizon #DigitalInfrastructure #ConnectingAustralia
We're excited for @Nikos Katinakis to join the Vocus team!

We're celebrating 20 years with @Darin Bodilly!   From the early days of dial‑up to the digital world we know today, Darin’s journey with Vocus is one for the history books.   Fun fact – Darin’s career at iPrimus (a Vocus brand) actually began in 1996 as Network Operations Centre Engineer and led him to numerous roles across iPrimus in Australia, Spain and the UK.   Darin moved back home in 2006 and re-joined the Australian business as VoIP Network Engineer for iPrimus and has since progressed to VoIP Voice Engineer (for then M2 Telecommunications) and to his current role today as a Senior Voice Engineer at Vocus.   Through several acquisitions, countries and roles spanning almost 30 years, what stands out to him the most isn’t the evolving industry, but the people he's worked with – some (pictured) are still celebrating Vocus milestones together today.   “It’s been an amazing journey shaped by the people along the way. I still remember coming back to Vocus in Australia after 10 years overseas and seeing many of the same faces I did all those years before. It was brilliant – we just picked up where we left off, and we’re still good friends and colleagues today, another 20 years on.”   Proof that great teams and strong relationships really stand the test of time.   Congrats to Darin on his (officially) 20 year and (unofficially) 30ish-year milestone! #TeamVocus

Shadow AI is already in most Australian workplaces. Today we launched Vocus Secure Shield with Fortinet to help organisations get visibility and help staff use AI appropriately. #DigitalInfrastructure #ConnectingAustralia
